mechanism_narrative: Sustained near-zero FEDFUNDS held at 0.16% for an extended period reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and encourages a persistent reach-for-yield rotation into equities, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the rate remains pinned near the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2012-06-01, unchanged from prior vintage
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE that exceeds 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises consensus expectations of corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp versus prior month's vintage value
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% as of 2012-06-01, unchanged from prior month
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where UNRATE fell by >0.2pp, subsequent 20-day forward returns were negative, or rank IC is below 0.03 across walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in HOUST and RSAFS signals a broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery that reinforces GDP growth expectations; only when both housing starts and retail sales rise together does the demand recovery signal become reliable enough to drive S&P 500 returns higher over a quarterly horizon, since either series alone is noisy.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS are jointly rising, 60-day forward returns are not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as long as the trend continues, independent of any threshold or regime condition.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO trends are not distinguishable from negative INDPRO trend periods (difference in means p > 0.05), or rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: While FEDFUNDS remains pinned at the zero lower bound, an unexpected acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation raises market fears of future policy tightening or stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors reprice discount rates upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.713 revised value with FEDFUNDS at 0.16%
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where this joint condition triggered, 5-day forward S&P 500 returns were positive, or rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increase in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE constitute a confirmed labor market strengthening signal that boosts short-term risk appetite, producing a positive S&P 500 return over the next trading week; either series alone is subject to revision noise and does not reliably predict the short-horizon move.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month-over-month AND UNRATE falls or holds steady in the same release
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134013 thousand persons revised value, UNRATE at 8.2%
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly met, 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month RSAFS increase exceeding 0.5% signals stronger-than-expected consumer spending momentum, which drives short-term bullish repositioning in equities and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 391376 million dollars as of 2012-05-01
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS surges above threshold are not significantly higher than the unconditional 5-day return distribution (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle slowdown regime identified by weak GDPC1 growth, a deceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth below zero signals contracting industrial output that foreshadows earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this mechanism is regime-specific because in early-recovery regimes the same INDPRO dip does not have predictive power.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 while trailing GDPC1 annualized growth is below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised value 17367.01 billion chained dollars as of 2012-01-01 vintage
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO declines within a low-GDP-growth regime are not significantly negative relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
